monitoring_requirements: Monitor archive success and lag continuously.
Fail closed if WAL archiving is missing, disabled, or falling behind the allowed recovery window.
Back up configuration files separately because PITR does not restore postgresql.conf, pg_hba.conf, or pg_ident.conf.

environment_rows.wal_archive_readiness_rule: A continuous WAL sequence must extend back at least to the start time of the latest admissible base backup.
restore_validation_steps: Restore latest base backup into an isolated drill cluster.
Replay archived WAL to the requested recovery target.
Verify control migration metadata and reader-window compatibility.
Verify audit continuity, including stream-head monotonicity and hash-chain posture where present.
Rebuild queue state from durable truth and verify no broker artifact is treated as legal truth.
Rebuild outstanding authority work from control plus audit stores and revalidate authority bindings.
Run restore privacy reconciliation before any non-operator reopen.

source_refs.rationale: The reference runtime topology names the primary control store and append-only audit store as separate bounded components.
Expand, backfill, and contract rules must remain machine-readable in the bootstrap topology.
Checkpoint, restore, queue rebuild, privacy reconciliation, and authority rebuild gates are mandatory for Tier 0 truth.
Audit ordering, append-only posture, and stream/hash-chain fields are defined here.
Canonical audit order must derive from audit_stream_ref and stream_sequence rather than wall-clock time.
The control store has to leave room for manifest, authority, submission, and restore metadata while the audit store preserves append-only event evidence.
ADR-002 already chose a relational-first control plane with an append-only audit store and left the exact product deferred.
The dependency register still marks PostgreSQL as a procurement or platform choice, so provider details must fail closed instead of being invented.
Separate control and audit credentials already exist and the store topology must align with them.

restore_gate_rows.requirement: Checkpoint cannot claim VERIFIED without bound restore drill evidence and verification basis.

source_refs.rationale: Restore readiness remains blocked until restore, privacy, audit, queue, and authority gates all pass.

restore_gate_rows.requirement: Checkpoint cannot reopen until privacy reconciliation evidence is bound and reopen-safe.

source_refs.rationale: Restore readiness remains blocked until restore, privacy, audit, queue, and authority gates all pass.

restore_gate_rows.requirement: Audit continuity must be verified before reopen.

source_refs.rationale: Restore readiness remains blocked until restore, privacy, audit, queue, and authority gates all pass.

restore_gate_rows.requirement: Queues must be rebuilt from durable truth before reopen.

source_refs.rationale: Restore readiness remains blocked until restore, privacy, audit, queue, and authority gates all pass.

restore_gate_rows.requirement: Outstanding authority work must be rebuilt from durable receipts and records.

source_refs.rationale: Restore readiness remains blocked until restore, privacy, audit, queue, and authority gates all pass.

restore_gate_rows.requirement: Authority binding lineage must be revalidated before authority-facing work resumes.

source_refs.rationale: Restore readiness remains blocked until restore, privacy, audit, queue, and authority gates all pass.

migration_window_policy.expand_posture: New schemas, columns, partitions, and roles may be added while prior readers still work.
migration_window_policy.backfill_posture: Backfills are idempotent, recorded, and replay-safe; they must not close the reader window early.
migration_window_policy.contract_posture: Contract/drop steps remain blocked until restore drills, replay readability, and supported reader windows all stay green.
migration_window_policy.rollback_boundary_policy: Rollback is allowed only while the prior reader window remains open and the destructive contract phase has not closed it.
migration_window_policy.fail_forward_policy: If destructive change or closed reader windows make rollback unsafe, the sanctioned path is a compensating forward fix.
migration_window_policy.replay_restore_policy: Restore and replay must be able to read the protected historical schema bundle set for any still-admissible checkpoint or manifest.
